notes: Thirty-first validated clue batch compiled from official school websites.
Clues intentionally avoid revealing enrollment size, grade span, or directional/proximity information because those are gameplay feedback channels.

clues.text: Its mission says the school teaches and inspires through a challenging curriculum that integrates technology, experiential learning, and critical thinking skills.
Official materials describe it as Edgecombe County's only charter school.
The school says it is one of only two North Carolina schools offering a PreK-12 agricultural program.
Official materials highlight the Rotary Club of Tarboro Animal Facility and the Edgecombe County Farm Bureau Agricultural Complex.
Staff pages list Dr. Mark Cockrell as Executive Director.
Board materials identify Anne Mann as chairman.
Its mission says the school provides a rigorous back-to-basics education with high personal expectations.
Official materials say the academic program uses Core Knowledge, Saxon Math, Shurley Grammar, and Core Essentials Character Education.
The school describes itself as a grassroots charter formed by concerned local community members.
Its public-facing welcome message refers to the campus as The Hive.
Principal profile materials identify Mr. Alexander Tiren as principal.
Official pages say the school was created to be a community staple and a meaningful educational choice for families in Caldwell County and surrounding areas.
Its mission says the school teaches and inspires each child to become an independent thinker and a contributing citizen with a life-long desire to learn.
Official materials say the curriculum is enriched with service learning and the Core Knowledge Sequence.
The school says success is not measured by state tests alone because it educates the whole child academically, morally, and socially.
Official materials emphasize character education and mastery of life skills as core parts of the program.
Administration pages list Brian Mathis as Executive Director.
The school's news and family resources are grouped under the label Knightly News.
Its public-facing slogan is Developing tomorrow's leaders today.
Official materials describe the school as a public school of choice with high expectations for all.
The school's overview says it creates value in the community by producing children who are independent thinkers and effective communicators.
Safety materials mention a dedicated TAMC Safety/Security Committee working with sheriff, police, and fire partners.
Official pages identify Dr. Dale Buie as Director of Education.
The homepage highlights strong external rankings from both Niche and U.S. News.
Its official parent organization uses the mission line, "to surround students with a community of support, empowering them to stay in school."
Official materials prominently celebrate student success in the NC Unity Conference writing contest.
The school's public materials spotlight science-class activities like student volcano work.
Its policy page publishes a broad set of school governance documents, including attendance, discipline, grievance, and wellness policies.
Staff pages identify Jonathan Blue as principal.
Official staff materials list Dr. Mazie Gibson as the school's counselor and student support specialist coordinator.
